notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: LHX breaks below the 52w low of $256.51 on rising volume, or the cohort rotation gap widens past -15pp — signaling the lag is idiosyncratic deterioration, not rotation, which would flip me toward neutral/trim.

positional.falsifier: Next earnings shows B/B falling below 0.95 with backlog YoY turning negative, or op margin deteriorating further with a supply-chain root cause — that confirms the drawdown is fundamental and flips me toward trim.

strategic.falsifier: A shift in DoD budget signal to sequester_risk or CR-driven cuts, or a geopolitical de-escalation toward a peace-dividend narrative — either would turn the structural tailwind into a headwind and push my score meaningfully positive.

tactical.falsifier: A single-day drop of >5% on non-fundamental news (e.g., China export-control headline) would flip me to a tactical buy; a blow-off spike RSI>75 into a weak book-to-bill print would flip me to trim.

positional.falsifier: A close below the 50-day ($1690) that breaks the trend, or a >15% drawdown from highs, would flip positional to a buy; a decisive breakdown through the 200-day would flip to trim.

strategic.falsifier: TSMC + Samsung + Intel all cutting 2026 capex, book-to-bill falling <1.0, or a hard China export ban would flip strategic decisively to trim (higher positive); a >15% drawdown or forward P/E dropping under 25x on accelerating TSMC revenue would flip it to a buy (negative).

tactical.falsifier: A sharp VIX spike above ~22 with SPY drawdown that triggers a genuine flight-to-quality (LIN outperforming SPY by >3pp over 10d) would flip this to a clear tactical buy.

positional.falsifier: A break below the 50-day (~$512.83) on a widening BBB OAS (>~120bps) signaling backlog/IRR deterioration would flip this positive/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation via next earnings that op margin compression and ROCE decline are structural (margins staying sub-28% with negative price/mix ex-FX and negative backlog growth) would validate the quant's sell lean and flip me clearly positive; conversely a >$1B H2 mega-project win or margin reversion to >30% would push me firmly negative.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 35 with an accelerating volume spike (true capitulation) would deepen the buy signal (more negative); conversely a decisive reclaim of the 50d SMA at $402 on Azure re-acceleration news would neutralize the drawdown-buy setup.

positional.falsifier: A weekly close back above the 50d SMA ($402) alongside forward-20d realized returns turning positive would validate the deep-value buy; conversely a break below the 52w low of $349.20 on capex-ROI concerns would flip me toward trimming.

strategic.falsifier: An Azure CC print decelerating below 22% with margin compression from capex blowout would flip strategic to neutral/trim; an OpenAI deal renegotiation favorable to MSFT (more IP access, cleaner governance) would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sudden 10Y move to ~4.9%+ (or a sharp drop to sub-4.2%) that mechanically drives ENB out of its range would justify a non-zero tactical score.

positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps (via a 10Y drop or an ENB price decline) would flip this to a BUY; conversely a dividend coverage scare would deepen the trim.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained rate-cut path pushing the 10Y meaningfully below ~4% (re-widening ENB's relative yield appeal) or a major sanctioned growth project would flip strategic to a BUY; a dividend cut or pipeline obsolescence event would deepen the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below the 200-day SMA ($12.65) on rising volume would flip me to trim-side tactically; conversely a reclaim of the $15 50-day would push tactical negative.

positional.falsifier: HY OAS widening decisively (e.g. >4bps in this scale / credit-spread blowout) alongside copper breaking down would flip positional to trim-side; sustained copper >$4.50/lb with GLNCY reclaiming the 50-day would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the copper demand cycle is structurally rolling over (sustained China copper import decline + inventory build + falling LME price) would flip strategic to trim; a coal-asset demerger or major buyback announcement would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A daily close decisively below the $32.84 52w low on rising volume would flip this from a dip-buy to a momentum-short, pushing the tactical score positive.

positional.falsifier: ETF flows turning decisively net-positive for a sustained stretch AND a reclaim of the 50DMA ($39.33) would confirm the bottom and justify moving toward the quant's max-conviction -100; conversely a clean break of $32.84 flips me toward neutral/positive.

strategic.falsifier: Clear evidence the 4-year cycle has structurally broken (either a sustained new regime of institutional accumulation OR a confirmed multi-quarter structural top with declining ETF AUM) would push this off neutral.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp MXN move (>2% intraday) on a Banxico surprise or US rate shock, or KOF gapping through SMA200 on volume, would flip the tactical read.

positional.falsifier: MXN breaking above ~18.5 on a sustained basis or a break below SMA200 ($98.60) would flip the positional bias to trim.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN regime shift to 19+ (structural peso weakness) or a margin miss from input-cost inflation would flip strategic to neutral/trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close above the 50-day ($18.53) on volume (flip bearish/neutral to buy momentum) OR a break below the 200-day ($15.84) with WTI cracking $65 (flip to trim on trend breakdown).

positional.falsifier: A dividend policy change (cut to base dividend, or diversion of extraordinary payouts to capex/social spending) would flip this positive/trim; conversely a confirmed reaffirmation of the shareholder-return framework at Q2 results would deepen the buy case.

strategic.falsifier: Concrete government action that either raids capital returns (windfall tax, forced non-economic capex, dividend override) would push the score firmly positive; conversely a credible governance ring-fence or oil moving structurally higher on OPEC+ discipline would flip it negative.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive Drewry WCI break above its recent range (rates trending up two-plus consecutive weeks) with price reclaiming and holding above the $12.20 50-day would flip this to a clearer buy; conversely a confirmed Suez reopening cratering rates flips it to sell.

positional.falsifier: A sustained GSCPI drop below 0.5 combined with Drewry WCI falling back toward pre-Suez levels would remove the freight tailwind and flip positional to a trim; a rate re-acceleration with price breaking $12.50 would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the Logistics segment margins are structurally deteriorating (repeated guidance cuts) plus P/B rising above 1.0 on a freight peak would remove the value case and flip strategic to neutral/trim; a demand collapse forcing dividend/buyback cuts would flip it firmly positive.

tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing above 72-75 with the drawdown closing to under 10% (approaching prior highs) would remove the setup discount and flip tactical to neutral/trim.

positional.falsifier: A break back below SMA50 (~$479) on weak peer bioprocessing reads would signal the cycle-bottom trade is failing and flip positional toward neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that organic growth re-accelerates toward 5%+ with margin recovery (Q1 2026 print or peer reads) would deepen the buy; conversely, evidence of a NEW leg down in bioprocessing (peers guiding to further destocking) plus NIH/China simultaneous cuts would flip strategic positive/trim.

tactical.falsifier: Price closing back below the 50-day SMA ($102.26) on expanding volume, or RSI rolling below 40, would flip this from buy-side to trim-side by confirming the bounce failed.

positional.falsifier: A reclaim of the 200-day SMA ($130.44) with positive EPS revision trend would confirm the buy; conversely, a new 52w low below $81.24 or a cRPO print <18% would flip me to trim-side.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed cRPO re-acceleration above ~22% alongside real rates falling below 0.5% would flip this negative (strategic buy); conversely, cRPO decel below 18% with AI-cost margin compression would flip it decisively positive.

tactical.falsifier: A break back below the 200-SMA ($529.43) on rising volume, or twin spread swinging to MA lagging V by >4pp, would flip this from neutral toward a buy setup (or confirm a tactical break).

positional.falsifier: Two consecutive quarters of cross-border volume growth below 6%, or EPS estimate revisions turning negative over 30/90d, would flip this to a trim.

strategic.falsifier: Disruption evidence advancing from anecdotal to 'material' (documented real-economy merchant volume displacement), OR interchange/network-fee legislation advancing to a floor vote / passing, would flip this to a trim.

tactical.falsifier: July 16 print showing procedure growth below 12% paired with explicit management commentary of US procedure-share loss to Hugo, OR the 2027 transition quantified as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue) — either flips tactical to trim.

positional.falsifier: A negative EPS revision cycle (90d estimates cut) confirming decelerating forward earnings, OR I&A growth decoupling below procedure growth for a second quarter — would flip positional toward trim.

strategic.falsifier: Management quantifying the 2027 transition as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue), or measurable US installed-account displacement by Hugo — would break the strategic moat thesis and flip the sign.
